subject: import better motion correction parameters?
body:
Hi Dmitry, I'm trying to use WARP with a data set where the beam shift a 
bit unstable, and "jumps" by large amounts in a seemingly random fashion. 
We got MotionCorr2 to give us decent alignments, but within WARP there are 
frequent failures (i.e. diagnostic images that are "ghosted" or blurry). It 
is a valuable enough data set (time and $$$$ to re-collect) that it would 
be worth a bit of time to get this working. 

Looking in the warp-generated XML files, I can see where the shift 
parameters are stored. It would be easy for me to write a script to "hack" 
WARP by inserting our MotionCorr2 shifts into the XML files after WARP 
generates its own (mis)alignments. Will that be enough to fool WARP, or is 
that just going to make it unhappy?

best, Chuck
